title: chamaemelum millefolium
intro: Chamaemelum nobile, commonly known as chamomile, is an annual or perennial plant in the daisy family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The medicinal properties of chamomile are attributed to its essential oils, primarily apigenin and luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of chamomile include promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, soothing digestive issues, and alleviating insomnia, as well as its use as a topical treatment for minor skin irritations and wounds. Chamomile can be prepared as a herbal tea, oil, or tincture, and is often used in aromatherapy to promote a sense of calm and well-being.
